g.data-narration: Welcome to Embedded System Hardware. We will explore the fundamental components that make up modern embedded computing devices.
An embedded system is a specialized computer designed for dedicated functions within larger mechanical or electrical systems.
g.text: A specialized computing system designed to perform specific, dedicated functions
within larger mechanical or electrical systems

g.data-narration: Microprocessors require external memory and peripherals, while microcontrollers integrate all components into a single chip for embedded applications.
The CPU executes instructions and controls hardware components. Common architectures include ARM, AVR, PIC, offering trade-offs between performance, power, and cost.
Memory stores program code and data. Volatile RAM loses data without power; non-volatile Flash and EEPROM retain data for permanent storage.
Digital I/O handles basic on/off signals. ADC converts analog sensor signals to digital; DAC produces analog outputs for controlling devices.
UART provides point-to-point async communication. I2C uses two wires for multi-device buses. SPI offers high-speed synchronous communication.
g.text: TX, RX lines | No clock needed

g.data-narration: Power management includes voltage regulators and power modes. Sleep modes and clock gating reduce consumption for battery-powered applications.
Timers generate precise delays, measure frequencies, and create PWM signals. Timer interrupts enable efficient time-sensitive task execution.
Interrupts allow immediate response to external events without polling. ISR handles the event while CPU suspends normal execution.
g.text: Keep ISRs short | Configurable priority | Vector table | Global interrupt enable

g.data-narration: Temperature monitoring uses ADC to read LM35 sensor output (10mV/°C) and displays the converted temperature value via LCD or UART.
Motor control uses PWM to vary effective voltage to a DC motor. Timer generates PWM at fixed frequency with variable duty cycle for speed control.
g.text: Input: Potentiometer (ADC) or UART commands adjust duty cycle for speed control

g.data-narration: I2C communication uses SDA and SCL lines with pull-up resistors. Master initiates START, sends address, writes register, then reads data.
g.text: START → Address(W) → ACK → Reg Addr → START → Address(R) → ACK → Data bytes → STOP

g.data-narration: Embedded systems combine CPU, memory, I/O, and communication peripherals. Microcontrollers integrate these for efficient embedded applications.
